metric_definition.formula: aligned_frames / eligible_frames where eligible = frames with preferred_lane != null
metric_definition.denominator: valid non-fallback frames that have a preferred_lane in route_context_summary

stub_behavior_root_cause.description: stub_v1 sets target_lane from baseline_context.get('target_lane', 'current'), NOT from route_context.preferred_lane. This is the primary source of alignment variance.
stub_behavior_root_cause.consequence: When baseline_context.target_lane == 'current' but route_context.preferred_lane == 'right' or 'left', the stub emits target_lane='current', which the metric counts as aligned ONLY IF preferred_lane == 'current'. When preferred_lane != 'current', it counts as misaligned.
stub_behavior_root_cause.synthetic_frame_aggravation: Synthetic frames from frozen corpus (used where stage3b_behavior_timeline is missing) hardcode target_lane='current' regardless of corridor.preferred_lane. This maximally deflates alignment on keep_right / keep_left cases.
stub_behavior_root_cause.critical_distinction: This is NOT a tactical decision error. The agent stub never reads preferred_lane from route_context to set target_lane. The mapping gap is in the adapter stub design, not in the contract or in real agent reasoning.

stub_limit.description: Misalignment caused entirely by stub_v1 not consuming route_context.preferred_lane when setting target_lane. Would be fixed by improving the stub's target_lane selection heuristic. Does NOT indicate real agent tactical error.

route_context_issue.description: Misalignment caused by preferred_lane being null or 'current' in route_context_summary, making the frame ineligible for alignment calculation. Reduces eligible_frame denominator and creates misleading rate computation.
route_context_issue.severity: soft_warning_acceptable — reduces denominator, doesn't indicate real mismatch
route_context_issue.fix_owner: ensure route_context preferred_lane is propagated from Stage 3B timeline into behavior_request_v2 bundle

contract_mapping_issue.description: Misalignment caused by synthetic frame generation using target_lane='current' while corridor preferred_lane is 'right'. Not a real agent decision — artifact of synthetic fallback when stage3b_behavior_timeline is missing.
cases_affected: ml_right_positive_core (current 2-case list when running on synthetic frames)
contract_mapping_issue.severity: soft_warning_acceptable — will resolve when live stage3b timelines are wired
contract_mapping_issue.fix_owner: wire stage3b_behavior_timeline artifacts into case specs; or fix _synthesize_frames_from_frozen() to use corridor.preferred_lane as target_lane

true_tactical_mismatch.description: Misalignment caused by real agent disagreement with route-conditioned lane intent. Only possible when: (1) live stage3b timeline is present, (2) baseline emits keep_right / keep_left intent, (3) agent emits different target_lane. With stub_v1 this category cannot be populated because stub mirrors baseline target_lane directly.
true_tactical_mismatch.stub_v1_note: No true_tactical_mismatch events possible with stub_v1. This category is only meaningful when LLM adapter is wired.
true_tactical_mismatch.severity: NOT A CURRENT WARNING — future concern for LLM adapter runs

ml_right_positive_core.explanation: Synthetic frames hardcode target_lane='current'. Metric counts preferred_lane='current' OR preferred_lane==agent_lane. In synthetic generation, preferred_lane is also set to 'current' (from corridor fallback). So alignment = 1.0 trivially, not because agent correctly selected 'right'. This is a false-positive alignment: the metric passes, but the pass is meaningless with synthetic frames.
ml_right_positive_core.deviation_run_to_run_stability: deterministic — same synthetic frame pattern every run

stop_follow_ambiguity_core.explanation: preferred_lane='current' → metric aligned condition is satisfied by definition (preferred == 'current' is always aligned). alignment=1.0 is accurate but uninformative — it cannot distinguish real route consumption from the stub's default.

arbitration_stop_during_prepare_right.expected_alignment_with_live_timeline: HIGH during prepare_lane_change_right phase (target_lane='right'). CRITICAL RISK during arbitration phase: baseline drops LC → target_lane reverts to 'current'. Stub mirrors this correctly. Alignment rate will track baseline faithfully.
arbitration_stop_during_prepare_right.alignment_expected_value_range: 0.3 to 0.7 depending on frame split between prepare-LC and stop phases
arbitration_stop_during_prepare_right.warning_category: stub_limit — partial misalignment during stop phase is expected (LC cancelled, preferred_lane='right' but target_lane='current'). This is the most analytically interesting case.
arbitration_stop_during_prepare_right.deviation_run_to_run_stability: deterministic with live timeline, stable pattern

adjacent_right_unsafe_hold.stub_target_lane_source: baseline_context.target_lane — will be 'current' for stop/follow intents regardless of preferred_lane='right'
adjacent_right_unsafe_hold.expected_alignment_with_live_timeline: LOW — preferred_lane='right', but baseline emits stop/follow which sets target_lane='current'. Metric will count these frames as misaligned. This produces systematic low alignment ~0.0 to 0.3 for all 60 frames.
adjacent_right_unsafe_hold.warning_category: stub_limit — agent correctly mirrors stop/follow from baseline, but route alignment metric penalizes because agent emits target_lane='current' while preferred_lane='right'. This is a metric design limitation for stop-dominated cases, not a real tactical error.
adjacent_right_unsafe_hold.deviation_run_to_run_stability: deterministic and stable — will fail alignment threshold every run
adjacent_right_unsafe_hold.important_note: adjacent_right_unsafe_hold has status=provisional. It is NOT in the current 4-case expansion set. Analysis here is prospective.

which_case_deviates_most.reason: Stop-dominated case with preferred_lane='right'. Stub emits target_lane='current' for all stop/follow intents. Systematic 100% misalignment on eligible frames.
which_case_deviates_most.note: ml_right_positive_core currently shows 1.0 (false positive from synthetic frames, not real signal)

stability_verdict.explanation: stub_v1 is fully deterministic. Synthetic frame generation is deterministic. All alignment patterns will repeat identically across every run. There is no stochastic component. Run-to-run variance = 0.
stability_verdict.implication: A 10-run campaign will show identical alignment values every run. Variance = 0 is expected and correct for stub_v1.

dominant_misaligning_intent.mechanism: Baseline emits stop/follow with target_lane='current'. Stub mirrors this. Preferred_lane='right' (from route). Metric counts as misaligned.

fix_stub_adapter.description: Stub should use route_context.preferred_lane to derive target_lane when baseline intent is not a LC intent. This removes the stub_limit warning category.
fix_stub_adapter.priority: MEDIUM — before LLM adapter runs, not critical for contract validation

fix_synthetic_frame_builder.description: _synthesize_frames_from_frozen() should set target_lane = corridor.preferred_lane, not 'current'. This fixes the false-positive 1.0 alignment on ml_right and similar cases.
fix_synthetic_frame_builder.priority: HIGH — current 1.0 alignment is a measurement artifact, not real signal

accept_stop_case_low_alignment.description: For stop-dominated cases (adjacent_right_unsafe_hold, arbitration_stop stop-phase), systematic alignment < threshold is structurally expected. Recommend per-case threshold override or exemption for frames with stop/follow intent.
accept_stop_case_low_alignment.priority: MEDIUM — design metric exemption for stop-intent frames

wire_live_stage3b_timelines.description: Eliminates the contract_mapping_issue category entirely. Stage 7C 2-case run currently uses synthetic frames — live timeline would fix the false-positive alignment on ml_right.
